Error Pattern Definitions (error_test_patterns.go):\n8\t//   - Predefined error scenarios for common S3 errors (404, 403, 500, etc.)\n9\t//   - Type-safe error structures (S3Error, ErrorScenarioConfig)\n10\t//   - Error categorization by type (Auth, NotFound, Internal, etc.)\n11\t//   - Mapping functions for error codes to patterns\n12\t//\n13\t// 2. HTTP Error Fixtures (http_error_fixtures.go):\n14\t//   - Reusable HTTP error response fixtures\n15\t//   - Configurable factory functions for common error codes\n16\t//   - XML serialization for S3-compatible error responses\n17\t//\n18\t// 3. Test Infrastructure (error_test_infrastructure_test.go):\n19\t//   - Test server and credential fixtures\n20\t//   - Reusable test helpers for error validation\n21\t//   - Setup/teardown utilities\n22\t//\n23\t// 4. Status Code Validation (error_status_validation.go):\n24\t//   - Comprehensive status code validation helpers\n25\t//   - Flexible assertion modes (boolean vs detailed error)\n26\t//   - Multi-code validation support\n27\t//\n28\t// 5. Test Pattern Tables (error_test_patterns_base_test.go):\n29\t//   - Table-driven test structures for consistency\n30\t//   - Organized test cases by error category\n31\t//   - Extensible patterns for custom scenarios\n32\t//\n33\t// # Quick Start\n34\t//\n35\t// Use a predefined pattern:\n36\t//\n37\t//\tpattern := CommonErrorPatterns.ResourceNotFound\n38\t//\tfmt.Printf(\"Expected status: %d\\n\", pattern.ExpectedStatus) // 404\n39\t//\n40\t// Get a pattern by error code:\n41\t//\n42\t//\tpattern := PatternForCode(\"NoSuchKey\")\n43\t//\tfmt.Printf(\"Pattern: %s\\n\", pattern.Name)\n44\t//\n45\t// Get all patterns for a category:\n46\t//\n47\t//\tauthPatterns := PatternsForCategory(CategoryAuth)\n48\t//\tfor _, p := range authPatterns {\n49\t//\t    fmt.Printf(\"- %s\\n\", p.Name)\n50\t//\t}\n51\t//\n52\t// Create a test fixture:\n53\t//\n54\t//\tfixture := NotFoundFixture(\"/api/blobs/missing-file.txt\")\n55\t//\tresponse := fixture.ToXMLResponse()\n56\t//\n57\t// # Error Pattern Categories\n58\t//\n59\t//   - CommonErrorPatterns: 8 patterns covering the most common S3 error scenarios\n60\t//   - AuthErrorPatterns: 6 patterns specifically for authentication failures\n61\t//   - ClientErrorPatterns: 4 patterns for 4xx client errors\n62\t//   - ServerErrorPatterns: 2 patterns for 5xx server errors\n63\t//   - ErrorPatternByCategory: Map-based access to patterns by category\n64\t//\n65\t// # Type Safety\n66\t//\n67\t// All error structures use consistent, well-documented types:\n68\t//   - S3Error: Core S3 error response structure\n69\t//   - ErrorScenarioConfig: Test scenario configuration\n70\t//   - ErrorResponseFixture: Complete response fixture with metadata\n71\t//   - HTTPErrorFixture: HTTP-specific error fixture\n72\t//   - ErrorResponseMetadata: Response metadata for logging/debugging\n73\t//\n74\t// # Testing Best Practices\n75\t//\n76\t// 1. Use predefined patterns when possible for consistency\n77\t// 2. Extend patterns with custom configurations rather than creating from scratch\n78\t// 3. Use category-based pattern access for organized testing\n79\t// 4. Leverage table-driven tests for comprehensive coverage\n80\t// 5. Document custom patterns with clear descriptions\n81\t//\n82\t// # Related Files\n83\t//\n84\t//   - error_pattern_usage_example.go: Comprehensive usage examples\n85\t//   - error_test_patterns_test.go: Tests for the pattern infrastructure\n86\t//   - error_structure_validation_test.go: Error structure validation tests\n87\t//   - error_status_validation_test.go: Status code validation tests\n88\t//   - error_response_verification_test.go: Response verification tests\n89\tpackage server\n90\t\n91\timport (\n92\t\t\"encoding/xml\"\n93\t\t\"io\"\n94\t\t\"net/http\"\n95\t\t\"time\"\n96\t)\n97\t\n98\t// =============================================================================\n99\t// ERROR TEST PATTERNS - BASE TYPES\n100\t// =============================================================================\n101\t// This file provides foundational types and structures for error response testing.\n102\t// It defines the common error structures that are used across both test and\n103\t// non-test code, ensuring type consistency and reusability.\n104\t//\n105\t// Design Philosophy:\n106\t// - Separation of concerns: base types in this file, test-specific helpers in test files\n107\t// - Type consistency: shared types across test and non-test code\n108\t// - Reusability: fixtures and patterns can be used anywhere\n109\t// - Self-documenting: clear field names and comprehensive documentation\n110\t//\n111\t// Usage:\n112\t//   - Test files import this package for base error structures\n113\t//   - Non-test files (fixtures) use S3Error for type-safe error responses\n114\t//   - Test patterns build on these base types\n115\t// =============================================================================\n116\t\n117\t// =============================================================================\n118\t// CORE ERROR TYPES\n119\t// =============================================================================\n120\t\n121\t// S3Error represents an S3 XML error response.\n122\t//\n123\t// This is the core type used across error testing, fixtures, and validation.\n124\t// It matches the standard S3 error response format with Code and Message fields.\n125\t//\n126\t// Use this type when:\n127\t//   - Parsing S3 XML error responses\n128\t//   - Creating test fixtures that simulate S3 errors\n129\t//   - Validating error response structure\n130\t//   - Serializing errors to XML format\n131\t//\n132\t// Example (parsing):\n133\t//\n134\t//\tvar s3Err S3Error\n135\t//\terr := xml.Unmarshal(responseBody, &s3Err)\n136\t//\tif err != nil {\n137\t//\t    return err\n138\t//\t}\n139\t//\tfmt.Printf(\"Error code: %s, message: %s\\n\", s3Err.Code, s3Err.Message)\n140\t//\n141\t// Example (creating):\n142\t//\n143\t//\ts3Err := S3Error{\n144\t//\t    Code: \"NoSuchKey\",\n145\t//\t    Message: \"The specified key does not exist\",\n146\t//\t}\n147\t//\txmlBytes, _ := xml.Marshal(s3Err)\n148\t//\n149\t// Fields:\n150\t//   - XMLName: XML element name (set to \"Error\" automatically)\n151\t//   - Code: S3 error code (e.g., \"NoSuchKey\", \"AccessDenied\")\n152\t//   - Message: Human-readable error message\n153\ttype S3Error struct {\n154\t\tXMLName xml.Name `xml:\"Error\"`\n155\t\tCode    string   `xml:\"Code\"`\n156\t\tMessage string   `xml:\"Message\"`\n157\t}\n158\t\n159\t// =============================================================================\n160\t// ERROR SCENARIO TYPES (for non-test fixture usage)\n161\t// =============================================================================\n162\t\n163\t// ErrorScenarioConfig represents configuration for an error test scenario.\n164\t//\n165\t// This type is the primary configuration structure for defining expected error\n166\t// behaviors in tests. It is used across test fixtures, validation helpers, and\n167\t// predefined error patterns.\n168\t//\n169\t// Use this type when:\n170\t//   - Defining expected error behaviors for test scenarios\n171\t//   - Configuring validation rules for error responses\n172\t//   - Creating custom error patterns based on predefined ones\n173\t//   - Extending existing patterns with custom expectations\n174\t//\n175\t// Example (using a predefined pattern):\n176\t//\n177\t//\tpattern := CommonErrorPatterns.ResourceNotFound\n178\t//\tfmt.Printf(\"Expected status: %d\\n\", pattern.ExpectedStatus) // 404\n179\t//\tfmt.Printf(\"Expected code: %s\\n\", pattern.ExpectedCode) // \"NoSuchKey\"\n180\t//\n181\t// Example (creating a custom pattern):\n182\t//\n183\t//\tcustomPattern := ErrorScenarioConfig{\n184\t//\t    Name: \"Custom Not Found\",\n185\t//\t    ExpectedCode: \"CustomNotFound\",\n186\t//\t    ExpectedStatus: 404,\n187\t//\t    ExpectedMessage: \"Custom not found message\",\n188\t//\t    ExpectedKeywords: []string{\"custom\", \"not\", \"found\"},\n189\t//\t    MinMessageLength: 15,\n190\t//\t    MaxResponseTime: 500 * time.Millisecond,\n191\t//\t    Description: \"Custom not found scenario\",\n192\t//\t    Category: string(CategoryNotFound),\n193\t//\t}\n194\t//\n195\t// Example (extending a predefined pattern):\n196\t//\n197\t//\tbasePattern := CommonErrorPatterns.ResourceNotFound\n198\t//\tcustomPattern := basePattern\n199\t//\tcustomPattern.ExpectedMessage = \"Custom message\"\n200\t//\tcustomPattern.MaxResponseTime = 1000 * time.Millisecond\n201\t//\n202\t// Fields:\n203\t//   - Name: Scenario name for identification and test reporting\n204\t//   - ExpectedCode: S3 error code (e.g., \"NoSuchKey\", \"AccessDenied\")\n205\t//   - ExpectedStatus: HTTP status code (e.g., 404, 403, 500)\n206\t//   - ExpectedMessage: Text that should appear in the error message\n207\t//   - ExpectedKeywords: Alternative keywords (any match is acceptable)\n208\t//   - MinMessageLength: Minimum acceptable message length\n209\t//   - MaxResponseTime: Maximum acceptable response duration\n210\t//   - Description: Explains what this scenario tests\n211\t//   - Category: Error type category (e.g., \"NotFound\", \"Auth\", \"CORS\")\n212\t//\n213\t// Default values:\n214\t//   - MinMessageLength: 15 (via DefaultErrorScenarioConfig())\n215\t//   - Category: \"General\" (via DefaultErrorScenarioConfig())\n216\t//   - MaxResponseTime: Varies by pattern (typically 200-1000ms)\n217\ttype ErrorScenarioConfig struct {\n218\t\t// Name is the scenario name for identification\n219\t\tName string\n220\t\n221\t\t// ExpectedCode is the expected S3 error code (e.g., \"NoSuchKey\", \"AccessDenied\")\n222\t\tExpectedCode string\n223\t\n224\t\t// ExpectedStatus is the expected HTTP status code\n225\t\tExpectedStatus int\n226\t\n227\t\t// ExpectedMessage contains text that should appear in the error message\n228\t\tExpectedMessage string\n229\t\n230\t\t// ExpectedKeywords are alternative keywords (any match is acceptable)\n231\t\tExpectedKeywords []string\n232\t\n233\t\t// MinMessageLength is the minimum acceptable message length\n234\t\tMinMessageLength int\n235\t\n236\t\t// MaxResponseTime is the maximum acceptable response duration\n237\t\tMaxResponseTime time.Duration\n238\t\n239\t\t// Description explains what this scenario tests\n240\t\tDescription string\n241\t\n242\t\t// Category categorizes the error type (e.g., \"NotFound\", \"Auth\", \"CORS\")\n243\t\tCategory string\n244\t}\n245\t\n246\t// DefaultErrorScenarioConfig returns a default scenario configuration.\n247\tfunc DefaultErrorScenarioConfig() ErrorScenarioConfig {\n248\t\treturn ErrorScenarioConfig{\n249\t\t\tMinMessageLength: 15,\n250\t\t\tCategory:         \"General\",\n251\t\t}\n252\t}\n253\t\n254\t// =============================================================================\n255\t// ERROR RESPONSE VALIDATION TYPES\n256\t// =============================================================================\n257\t\n258\t// ErrorResponseMetadata contains metadata about an error response.\n259\t//\n260\t// This type is used for logging, debugging, and performance monitoring. It captures\n261\t// all relevant information about an error response without storing the full body.\n262\t//\n263\t// Use this type when:\n264\t//   - Logging error responses for debugging\n265\t//   - Monitoring response times and performance\n266\t//   - Tracking error patterns over time\n267\t//   - Collecting metrics for error analysis\n268\t//\n269\t// Example (using ExtractMetadata):\n270\t//\n271\t//\tstart := time.Now()\n272\t//\tresp, err := http.Get(\"http://example.com/api/resource\")\n273\t//\tduration := time.Since(start)\n274\t//\n275\t//\tmetadata := ExtractMetadata(resp, duration)\n276\t//\tlog.Printf(\"Error: %s (%d) in %v\\n\", metadata.ErrorCode, metadata.StatusCode, metadata.ResponseTime)\n277\t//\n278\t// Fields:\n279\t//   - StatusCode: HTTP status code received\n280\t//   - ErrorCode: S3 error code parsed from the response\n281\t//   - Message: Error message text\n282\t//   - ResponseTime: How long the request took\n283\t//   - ContentLength: Size of the response body\n284\t//   - ContentType: Response content type header\n285\t//   - Timestamp: When the response was received\n286\ttype ErrorResponseMetadata struct {\n287\t\t// StatusCode is the HTTP status code received\n288\t\tStatusCode int\n289\t\n290\t\t// ErrorCode is the S3 error code parsed from the response\n291\t\tErrorCode string\n292\t\n293\t\t// Message is the error message text\n294\t\tMessage string\n295\t\n296\t\t// ResponseTime is how long the request took\n297\t\tResponseTime time.Duration\n298\t\n299\t\t// ContentLength is the size of the response body\n300\t\tContentLength int64\n301\t\n302\t\t// ContentType is the response content type header\n303\t\tContentType string\n304\t\n305\t\t// Timestamp when the response was received\n306\t\tTimestamp time.Time\n307\t}\n308\t\n309\t// ExtractMetadata extracts metadata from an HTTP response.\n310\t//\n311\t// This helper is useful for logging and debugging error responses. It parses\n312\t// the S3 error from the response body and returns comprehensive metadata.\n313\t//\n314\t// Parameters:\n315\t//   - resp: HTTP response to extract metadata from\n316\t//   - duration: Time taken for the request\n317\t//\n318\t// Returns:\n319\t//   - ErrorResponseMetadata containing all extracted information\n320\t//\n321\t// Use this helper when:\n322\t//   - Logging error responses for later analysis\n323\t//   - Debugging unexpected error responses\n324\t//   - Collecting performance metrics\n325\t//   - Tracking error patterns in production\n326\t//\n327\t// Note: This function consumes the response body.\n328\tfunc ExtractMetadata(resp *http.Response, duration time.Duration) ErrorResponseMetadata {\n329\t\tbody, _ := io.ReadAll(resp.Body)\n330\t\tdefer resp.Body.Close()\n331\t\n332\t\tvar s3Err S3Error\n333\t\txml.Unmarshal(body, &s3Err)\n334\t\n335\t\treturn ErrorResponseMetadata{\n336\t\t\tStatusCode:   resp.StatusCode,\n337\t\t\tErrorCode:    s3Err.Code,\n338\t\t\tMessage:      s3Err.Message,\n339\t\t\tResponseTime: duration,\n340\t\t\tContentLength: resp.ContentLength,\n341\t\t\tContentType:  resp.Header.Get(\"Content-Type\"),\n342\t\t\tTimestamp:    time.Now(),\n343\t\t}\n344\t}\n345\t\n346\t// =============================================================================\n347\t// ERROR FIXTURE TYPES\n348\t// =============================================================================\n349\t\n350\t// ErrorResponseFixture represents a complete error response fixture.\n351\t//\n352\t// This type combines the S3 error with HTTP response metadata for comprehensive\n353\t// testing scenarios. It is a complete representation of an error response that\n354\t// can be used for validation, comparison, and re-creation in tests.\n355\t//\n356\t// Use this type when:\n357\t//   - Creating complete error response fixtures for testing\n358\t//   - Comparing actual responses against expected fixtures\n359\t//   - Serializing and re-creating error responses in tests\n360\t//   - Capturing full response state for debugging\n361\t//\n362\t// Example (using ToFixture):\n363\t//\n364\t//\tstart := time.Now()\n365\t//\tresp, err := http.Get(\"http://example.com/api/resource\")\n366\t//\tduration := time.Since(start)\n367\t//\n368\t//\tfixture, err := ToFixture(resp, duration)\n369\t//\tif err != nil {\n370\t//\t    return err\n371\t//\t}\n372\t//\tfmt.Printf(\"Fixture: %s (%d)\\n\", fixture.S3Error.Code, fixture.HTTPStatus)\n373\t//\n374\t// Example (manual creation):\n375\t//\n376\t//\tfixture := &ErrorResponseFixture{\n377\t//\t    S3Error: S3Error{Code: \"NoSuchKey\", Message: \"Not found\"},\n378\t//\t    HTTPStatus: 404,\n379\t//\t    Headers: http.Header{\"Content-Type\": []string{\"application/xml\"}},\n380\t//\t    Body: []byte(\"<?xml version=\\\"1.0\\\"?><Error><Code>NoSuchKey</Code></Error>\"),\n381\t//\t    Duration: 150 * time.Millisecond,\n382\t//\t}\n383\t//\n384\t// Fields:\n385\t//   - S3Error: Parsed S3 error structure\n386\t//   - HTTPStatus: HTTP status code\n387\t//   - Headers: HTTP response headers\n388\t//   - Body: Raw response body bytes\n389\t//   - Duration: Response time\n390\ttype ErrorResponseFixture struct {\n391\t\t// S3Error is the parsed S3 error structure\n392\t\tS3Error S3Error\n393\t\n394\t\t// HTTPStatus is the HTTP status code\n395\t\tHTTPStatus int\n396\t\n397\t\t// Headers contains the HTTP response headers\n398\t\tHeaders http.Header\n399\t\n400\t\t// Body is the raw response body\n401\t\tBody []byte\n402\t\n403\t\t// Duration is how long the response took\n404\t\tDuration time.Duration\n405\t}\n406\t\n407\t// ToFixture converts an HTTP response to a fixture.\n408\t//\n409\t// This helper parses an HTTP response and creates a complete fixture that can\n410\t// be used for testing, validation, and debugging. It reads and parses the\n411\t// response body, extracts headers, and captures timing information.\n412\t//\n413\t// Parameters:\n414\t//   - resp: HTTP response to convert\n415\t//   - duration: Time taken for the request\n416\t//\n417\t// Returns:\n418\t//   - *ErrorResponseFixture containing all response information\n419\t//   - error if response body parsing fails\n420\t//\n421\t// Use this helper when:\n422\t//   - Capturing actual responses for fixture creation\n423\t//   - Creating test fixtures from real server responses\n424\t//   - Debugging unexpected error responses\n425\t//   - Recording responses for regression testing\n426\t//\n427\t// Note: This function consumes the response body.\n428\tfunc ToFixture(resp *http.Response, duration time.Duration) (*ErrorResponseFixture, error) {\n429\t\tbody, err := io.ReadAll(resp.Body)\n430\t\tif err != nil {\n431\t\t\treturn nil, err\n432\t\t}\n433\t\tdefer resp.Body.Close()\n434\t\n435\t\tvar s3Err S3Error\n436\t\tif err := xml.Unmarshal(body, &s3Err); err != nil {\n437\t\t\treturn nil, err\n438\t\t}\n439\t\n440\t\treturn &ErrorResponseFixture{\n441\t\t\tS3Error:   s3Err,\n442\t\t\tHTTPStatus: resp.StatusCode,\n443\t\t\tHeaders:   resp.Header.Clone(),\n444\t\t\tBody:      body,\n445\t\t\tDuration:  duration,\n446\t\t}, nil\n447\t}\n448\t\n449\t// =============================================================================\n450\t// ERROR CATEGORY ENUMERATION\n451\t// =============================================================================\n452\t\n453\t// ErrorCategory represents different categories of errors.\n454\t//\n455\t// Error categories are used to organize and group error patterns by type.\n456\t// They enable efficient testing and validation of specific error classes.\n457\t//\n458\t// Use error categories when:\n459\t//   - Getting all patterns for a specific error type via PatternsForCategory()\n460\t//   - Organizing tests by error category\n461\t//   - Filtering errors in monitoring and logging\n462\t//   - Understanding error patterns at a high level\n463\t//\n464\t// Example (using a category):\n465\t//\n466\t//\tauthPatterns := PatternsForCategory(CategoryAuth)\n467\t//\tfor _, pattern := range authPatterns {\n468\t//\t    fmt.Printf(\"Auth pattern: %s\\n\", pattern.Name)\n469\t//\t}\n470\t//\n471\t// Example (checking a category):\n472\t//\n473\t//\tcategory := CategoryForCode(\"NoSuchKey\")\n474\t//\tfmt.Printf(\"Category: %s\\n\", category) // \"NotFound\"\n475\ttype ErrorCategory string\n476\t\n477\tconst (\n478\t\t// CategoryAuth authentication errors (401, 403)\n479\t\t//\n480\t\t// Covers:\n481\t\t//   - Missing authentication tokens\n482\t\t//   - Invalid access keys\n483\t\t//   - Signature mismatches\n484\t\t//   - Expired requests\n485\t\tCategoryAuth ErrorCategory = \"Auth\"\n486\t\n487\t\t// CategoryNotFound resource not found errors (404)\n488\t\t//\n489\t\t// Covers:\n490\t\t//   - Missing objects (NoSuchKey)\n491\t\t//   - Missing buckets\n492\t\t//   - Missing resources\n493\t\tCategoryNotFound ErrorCategory = \"NotFound\"\n494\t\n495\t\t// CategoryInvalidRequest invalid request errors (400, 415)\n496\t\t//\n497\t\t// Covers:\n498\t\t//   - Malformed requests\n499\t\t//   - Invalid parameters\n500\t\t//   - Unsupported media types\n501\t\tCategoryInvalidRequest ErrorCategory = \"InvalidRequest\"\n502\t\n503\t\t// CategoryMethodNotAllowed method not allowed errors (405)\n504\t\t//\n505\t\t// Covers:\n506\t\t//   - Unsupported HTTP methods\n507\t\t//   - Method restrictions\n508\t\tCategoryMethodNotAllowed ErrorCategory = \"MethodNotAllowed\"\n509\t\n510\t\t// CategoryInternal server errors (500)\n511\t\t//\n512\t\t// Covers:\n513\t\t//   - Internal server errors\n514\t\t//   - Service unavailable\n515\t\t//   - Infrastructure failures\n516\t\tCategoryInternal ErrorCategory = \"Internal\"\n517\t\n518\t\t// CategoryCORS CORS-related errors\n519\t\t//\n520\t\t// Covers:\n521\t\t//   - CORS validation failures\n522\t\t//   - Origin restrictions\n523\t\tCategoryCORS ErrorCategory = \"CORS\"\n524\t\n525\t\t// CategoryGeneral general uncategorized errors\n526\t\t//\n527\t\t// Default category for errors that don't fit other categories\n528\t\tCategoryGeneral ErrorCategory = \"General\"\n529\t)\n530\t\n531\t// String returns the string representation of the error category.\n532\tfunc (e ErrorCategory) String() string {\n533\t\treturn string(e)\n534\t}\n535\t\n536\t// =============================================================================\n537\t// ERROR CODE CONSTANTS\n538\t// =============================================================================\n539\t\n540\t// Common S3 error codes used across testing.\n541\t//\n542\t// These constants represent standard S3 error codes. Using constants instead of\n543\t// string literals prevents typos and enables compile-time checking.\n544\t//\n545\t// Use these constants when:\n546\t//   - Creating custom error patterns\n547\t//   - Parsing error responses\n548\t//   - Writing validation logic\n549\t//   - Referencing S3 error codes in tests\n550\t//\n551\t// Example:\n552\t//\n553\t//\tpattern := ErrorScenarioConfig{\n554\t//\t    ExpectedCode: ErrorCodeNoSuchKey, // Use constant instead of \"NoSuchKey\"\n555\t//\t    ExpectedStatus: 404,\n556\t//\t}\n557\t//\n558\t// S3 Error Code Reference:\n559\t// https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/API/ErrorResponses.html\n560\tconst (\n561\t\t// ErrorCodeAccessDenied - Access denied (403)\n562\t\t//\n563\t\t// Client does not have access to the requested resource.\n564\t\t// Common causes:\n565\t\t//   - Invalid or expired credentials\n566\t\t//   - Insufficient permissions\n567\t\t//   - ACL restrictions\n568\t\tErrorCodeAccessDenied = \"AccessDenied\"\n569\t\n570\t\t// ErrorCodeInvalidAccessKeyId - Invalid access key (403)\n571\t\t//\n572\t\t// The AWS access key ID provided does not exist in our records.\n573\t\t// Common causes:\n574\t\t//   - Mistyped access key\n575\t\t//   - Access key that doesn't belong to any account\n576\t\tErrorCodeInvalidAccessKeyId = \"InvalidAccessKeyId\"\n577\t\n578\t\t// ErrorCodeSignatureDoesNotMatch - Signature mismatch (403)\n579\t\t//\n580\t\t// The request signature calculated by the server does not match the signature provided.\n581\t\t// Common causes:\n582\t\t//   - Incorrect secret key\n583\t\t//   - Tampered request\n584\t\t//   - Signing algorithm mismatch\n585\t\tErrorCodeSignatureDoesNotMatch = \"SignatureDoesNotMatch\"\n586\t\n587\t\t// ErrorCodeMissingAuthenticationToken - Missing auth header (403)\n588\t\t//\n589\t\t// Authorization header is missing or required date header is missing.\n590\t\t// Common causes:\n591\t\t//   - No Authorization header\n592\t\t//   - Missing X-Amz-Date header\n593\t\tErrorCodeMissingAuthenticationToken = \"MissingAuthenticationToken\"\n594\t\n595\t\t// ErrorCodeNoSuchKey - Resource not found (404)\n596\t\t//\n597\t\t// The specified key does not exist.\n598\t\t// Common causes:\n599\t\t//   - Object was deleted\n600\t\t//   - Wrong bucket/key name\n601\t\tErrorCodeNoSuchKey = \"NoSuchKey\"\n602\t\n603\t\t// ErrorCodeMethodNotAllowed - Method not allowed (405)\n604\t\t//\n605\t\t// The specified method is not allowed against this resource.\n606\t\t// Common causes:\n607\t\t//   - Wrong HTTP method for endpoint\n608\t\t//   - Method restrictions\n609\t\tErrorCodeMethodNotAllowed = \"MethodNotAllowed\"\n610\t\n611\t\t// ErrorCodeUnsupportedMediaType - Unsupported media type (415)\n612\t\t//\n613\t\t// The content type is not supported.\n614\t\t// Common causes:\n615\t\t//   - Wrong Content-Type header\n616\t\t//   - Unsupported format\n617\t\tErrorCodeUnsupportedMediaType = \"UnsupportedMediaType\"\n618\t\n619\t\t// ErrorCodeInvalidRequest - Invalid request (400)\n620\t\t//\n621\t\t// The request is invalid or malformed.\n622\t\t// Common causes:\n623\t\t//   - Missing required parameters\n624\t\t//   - Invalid query string\n625\t\t//   - Malformed request body\n626\t\tErrorCodeInvalidRequest = \"InvalidRequest\"\n627\t\n628\t\t// ErrorCodeInternalError - Internal server error (500)\n629\t\t//\n630\t\t// Internal server error occurred.\n631\t\t// Common causes:\n632\t\t//   - Server-side bug\n633\t\t//   - Infrastructure failure\n634\t\t//   - Unexpected error condition\n635\t\tErrorCodeInternalError = \"InternalError\"\n636\t\n637\t\t// ErrorCodeRequestExpired - Request timestamp expired (403)\n638\t\t//\n639\t\t// Request has expired (typically >15 minutes old).\n640\t\t// Common causes:\n641\t\t//   - Stale request\n642\t\t//   - Clock skew\n643\t\tErrorCodeRequestExpired = \"RequestExpired\"\n644\t)\n645\t\n646\t// =============================================================================\n647\t// ERROR CATEGORY MAPPING\n648\t// =============================================================================\n649\t\n650\t// CategoryForCode returns the error category for a given error code.\n651\t//\n652\t// This helper maps S3 error codes to their corresponding error categories,\n653\t// enabling organized testing and analysis by error type.\n654\t//\n655\t// Parameters:\n656\t//   - code: S3 error code (e.g., \"NoSuchKey\", \"AccessDenied\")\n657\t//\n658\t// Returns:\n659\t//   - ErrorCategory for the given error code\n660\t//\n661\t// Use this helper when:\n662\t//   - Categorizing errors for logging/monitoring\n663\t//   - Organizing tests by error category\n664\t//   - Understanding error types at runtime\n665\t//   - Filtering errors by category\n666\t//\n667\t// Example:\n668\t//\n669\t//\tcategory := CategoryForCode(\"NoSuchKey\")\n670\t//\tif category == CategoryNotFound {\n671\t//\t    fmt.Println(\"This is a not-found error\")\n672\t//\t}\n673\t//\n674\t// Category mappings:\n675\t//   - Auth: AccessDenied, InvalidAccessKeyId, SignatureDoesNotMatch,\n676\t//     MissingAuthenticationToken, RequestExpired\n677\t//   - NotFound: NoSuchKey\n678\t//   - MethodNotAllowed: MethodNotAllowed\n679\t//   - InvalidRequest: UnsupportedMediaType, InvalidRequest\n680\t//   - Internal: InternalError\n681\t//   - General: Any other error code\n682\tfunc CategoryForCode(code string) ErrorCategory {\n683\t\tswitch code {\n684\t\tcase ErrorCodeAccessDenied, ErrorCodeInvalidAccessKeyId,\n685\t\t     ErrorCodeSignatureDoesNotMatch, ErrorCodeMissingAuthenticationToken,\n686\t\t     ErrorCodeRequestExpired:\n687\t\t\treturn CategoryAuth\n688\t\tcase ErrorCodeNoSuchKey:\n689\t\t\treturn CategoryNotFound\n690\t\tcase ErrorCodeMethodNotAllowed:\n691\t\t\treturn CategoryMethodNotAllowed\n692\t\tcase ErrorCodeUnsupportedMediaType, ErrorCodeInvalidRequest:\n693\t\t\treturn CategoryInvalidRequest\n694\t\tcase ErrorCodeInternalError:\n695\t\t\treturn CategoryInternal\n696\t\tdefault:\n697\t\t\treturn CategoryGeneral\n698\t\t}\n699\t}\n700\t\n701\t// =============================================================================\n702\t// HTTP STATUS CODE MAPPING\n703\t// =============================================================================\n704\t\n705\t// ExpectedStatusCodeForCode returns the expected HTTP status code for an S3 error code.\n706\t//\n707\t// This helper maps S3 error codes to their corresponding HTTP status codes,\n708\t// enabling validation of error responses without hardcoding status values.\n709\t//\n710\t// Parameters:\n711\t//   - code: S3 error code (e.g., \"NoSuchKey\", \"AccessDenied\")\n712\t//\n713\t// Returns:\n714\t//   - Expected HTTP status code for the given error code\n715\t//\n716\t// Use this helper when:\n717\t//   - Validating error responses\n718\t//   - Creating test expectations\n719\t//   - Understanding error code to status code mappings\n720\t//   - Writing generic error validation logic\n721\t//\n722\t// Example:\n723\t//\n724\t//\texpectedStatus := ExpectedStatusCodeForCode(\"NoSuchKey\")\n725\t//\tif resp.StatusCode == expectedStatus {\n726\t//\t    fmt.Println(\"Status code is correct\")\n727\t//\t}\n728\t//\n729\t// Status code mappings:\n730\t//   - 403: AccessDenied, InvalidAccessKeyId, SignatureDoesNotMatch,\n731\t//     MissingAuthenticationToken, RequestExpired\n732\t//   - 404: NoSuchKey\n733\t//   - 405: MethodNotAllowed\n734\t//   - 415: UnsupportedMediaType\n735\t//   - 400: InvalidRequest\n736\t//   - 500: InternalError\n737\t//   - 500 (default): Any other error code\n738\tfunc ExpectedStatusCodeForCode(code string) int {\n739\t\tswitch code {\n740\t\tcase ErrorCodeAccessDenied, ErrorCodeInvalidAccessKeyId,\n741\t\t     ErrorCodeSignatureDoesNotMatch, ErrorCodeMissingAuthenticationToken,\n742\t\t     ErrorCodeRequestExpired:\n743\t\t\treturn 403\n744\t\tcase ErrorCodeNoSuchKey:\n745\t\t\treturn 404\n746\t\tcase ErrorCodeMethodNotAllowed:\n747\t\t\treturn 405\n748\t\tcase ErrorCodeUnsupportedMediaType:\n749\t\t\treturn 415\n750\t\tcase ErrorCodeInvalidRequest:\n751\t\t\treturn 400\n752\t\tcase ErrorCodeInternalError:\n753\t\t\treturn 500\n754\t\tdefault:\n755\t\t\treturn 500 // Default to internal error for unknown codes\n756\t\t}\n757\t}\n758\t\n759\t// =============================================================================\n760\t// PREDEFINED COMMON ERROR PATTERNS\n761\t// =============================================================================\n762\t// These predefined patterns represent common error scenarios that can be reused\n763\t// across different test files. Each pattern is fully documented and provides\n764\t// a complete ErrorScenarioConfig ready for use.\n765\t//\n766\t// Usage Examples:\n767\t//   // Use a predefined pattern directly\n768\t//   pattern := CommonErrorPatterns.ResourceNotFound\n769\t//   config := pattern.Config()\n770\t//\n771\t//   // Extend a predefined pattern\n772\t//   customPattern := CommonErrorPatterns.ResourceNotFound\n773\t//   customPattern.Config.ExpectedMessage = \"Custom not found message\"\n774\t//\n775\t//   // Create a new pattern based on a predefined one\n776\t//   myPattern := ErrorScenarioConfig{\n777\t//       Name: \"My custom pattern\",\n778\t//       ExpectedCode: CommonErrorPatterns.ResourceNotFound.Config.ExpectedCode,\n779\t//       ExpectedStatus: CommonErrorPatterns.ResourceNotFound.Config.ExpectedStatus,\n780\t//   }\n781\t// =============================================================================\n782\t\n783\t// CommonErrorPatterns provides a collection of predefined error patterns\n784\t// for common S3 error scenarios. These patterns are ready-to-use and cover\n785\t// the most frequent error cases encountered in testing.\n786\tvar CommonErrorPatterns = struct {\n787\t\t// ResourceNotFound covers the classic 404 error when a requested object does not exist\n788\t\tResourceNotFound ErrorScenarioConfig\n789\t\n790\t\t// AccessDenied covers authentication failures where credentials are invalid\n791\t\tAccessDenied ErrorScenarioConfig\n792\t\n793\t\t// InvalidRequest covers malformed requests with missing or invalid parameters\n794\t\tInvalidRequest ErrorScenarioConfig\n795\t\n796\t\t// UnsupportedMediaType covers content-type validation errors\n797\t\tUnsupportedMediaType ErrorScenarioConfig\n798\t\n799\t\t// MethodNotAllowed covers HTTP method validation errors\n800\t\tMethodNotAllowed ErrorScenarioConfig\n801\t\n802\t\t// InternalServerError covers server-side errors\n803\t\tInternalServerError ErrorScenarioConfig\n804\t\n805\t\t// SignatureMismatch covers AWS signature validation failures\n806\t\tSignatureMismatch ErrorScenarioConfig\n807\t\n808\t\t// RequestExpired covers timestamp validation errors\n809\t\tRequestExpired ErrorScenarioConfig\n810\t}{\n811\t\t// ResourceNotFound: Standard 404 error for missing objects\n812\t\tResourceNotFound: ErrorScenarioConfig{\n813\t\t\tName:              \"Resource Not Found\",\n814\t\t\tExpectedCode:      ErrorCodeNoSuchKey,\n815\t\t\tExpectedStatus:    404,\n816\t\t\tExpectedMessage:   \"The specified key does not exist\",\n817\t\t\tExpectedKeywords:  []string{\"not\", \"found\", \"exist\", \"no such key\"},\n818\t\t\tMinMessageLength:   15,\n819\t\t\tMaxResponseTime:    500 * time.Millisecond,\n820\t\t\tDescription:       \"Tests for requests attempting to access non-existent resources. Covers GET, HEAD, and other operations on missing objects.\",\n821\t\t\tCategory:          string(CategoryNotFound),\n822\t\t},\n823\t\n824\t\t// AccessDenied: Authentication failure with invalid credentials\n825\t\tAccessDenied: ErrorScenarioConfig{\n826\t\t\tName:              \"Access Denied\",\n827\t\t\tExpectedCode:      ErrorCodeAccessDenied,\n828\t\t\tExpectedStatus:    403,\n829\t\t\tExpectedMessage:   \"Access Denied\",\n830\t\t\tExpectedKeywords:  []string{\"access\", \"denied\", \"permission\", \"authorized\"},\n831\t\t\tMinMessageLength:   12,\n832\t\t\tMaxResponseTime:    300 * time.Millisecond,\n833\t\t\tDescription:       \"Tests for requests with invalid or missing credentials. Covers both missing Authorization headers and invalid access keys.\",\n834\t\t\tCategory:          string(CategoryAuth),\n835\t\t},\n836\t\n837\t\t// InvalidRequest: Malformed request parameters\n838\t\tInvalidRequest: ErrorScenarioConfig{\n839\t\t\tName:              \"Invalid Request\",\n840\t\t\tExpectedCode:      ErrorCodeInvalidRequest,\n841\t\t\tExpectedStatus:    400,\n842\t\t\tExpectedMessage:   \"The request is invalid\",\n843\t\t\tExpectedKeywords:  []string{\"invalid\", \"request\", \"malformed\"},\n844\t\t\tMinMessageLength:   15,\n845\t\t\tMaxResponseTime:    200 * time.Millisecond,\n846\t\t\tDescription:       \"Tests for requests with missing required parameters, invalid query strings, or malformed request bodies.\",\n847\t\t\tCategory:          string(CategoryInvalidRequest),\n848\t\t},\n849\t\n850\t\t// UnsupportedMediaType: Content-type validation error\n851\t\tUnsupportedMediaType: ErrorScenarioConfig{\n852\t\t\tName:              \"Unsupported Media Type\",\n853\t\t\tExpectedCode:      ErrorCodeUnsupportedMediaType,\n854\t\t\tExpectedStatus:    415,\n855\t\t\tExpectedMessage:   \"The content type is not supported\",\n856\t\t\tExpectedKeywords:  []string{\"content\", \"type\", \"supported\", \"media\"},\n857\t\t\tMinMessageLength:   20,\n858\t\t\tMaxResponseTime:    200 * time.Millisecond,\n859\t\t\tDescription:       \"Tests for requests with unsupported Content-Type headers. Used when the server requires specific content types (e.g., application/xml).\",\n860\t\t\tCategory:          string(CategoryInvalidRequest),\n861\t\t},\n862\t\n863\t\t// MethodNotAllowed: HTTP method validation error\n864\t\tMethodNotAllowed: ErrorScenarioConfig{\n865\t\t\tName:              \"Method Not Allowed\",\n866\t\t\tExpectedCode:      ErrorCodeMethodNotAllowed,\n867\t\t\tExpectedStatus:    405,\n868\t\t\tExpectedMessage:   \"The specified method is not allowed\",\n869\t\t\tExpectedKeywords:  []string{\"method\", \"allowed\", \"supported\"},\n870\t\t\tMinMessageLength:   20,\n871\t\t\tMaxResponseTime:    200 * time.Millisecond,\n872\t\t\tDescription:       \"Tests for requests using HTTP methods that are not supported for the given resource. Commonly tested with POST on object endpoints.\",\n873\t\t\tCategory:          string(CategoryMethodNotAllowed),\n874\t\t},\n875\t\n876\t\t// InternalServerError: Server-side errors\n877\t\tInternalServerError: ErrorScenarioConfig{\n878\t\t\tName:              \"Internal Server Error\",\n879\t\t\tExpectedCode:      ErrorCodeInternalError,\n880\t\t\tExpectedStatus:    500,\n881\t\t\tExpectedMessage:   \"Internal server error\",\n882\t\t\tExpectedKeywords:  []string{\"internal\", \"error\", \"server\"},\n883\t\t\tMinMessageLength:   15,\n884\t\t\tMaxResponseTime:    1000 * time.Millisecond,\n885\t\t\tDescription:       \"Tests for unexpected server-side errors. These typically indicate a bug or infrastructure issue that needs investigation.\",\n886\t\t\tCategory:          string(CategoryInternal),\n887\t\t},\n888\t\n889\t\t// SignatureMismatch: AWS signature validation failure\n890\t\tSignatureMismatch: ErrorScenarioConfig{\n891\t\t\tName:              \"Signature Does Not Match\",\n892\t\t\tExpectedCode:      ErrorCodeSignatureDoesNotMatch,\n893\t\t\tExpectedStatus:    403,\n894\t\t\tExpectedMessage:   \"The request signature we calculated does not match\",\n895\t\t\tExpectedKeywords:  []string{\"signature\", \"match\", \"calculated\"},\n896\t\t\tMinMessageLength:   25,\n897\t\t\tMaxResponseTime:    300 * time.Millisecond,\n898\t\t\tDescription:       \"Tests for requests with incorrect AWS signature calculations. Covers malformed signatures, tampered requests, and incorrect secret keys.\",\n899\t\t\tCategory:          string(CategoryAuth),\n900\t\t},\n901\t\n902\t\t// RequestExpired: Timestamp validation error\n903\t\tRequestExpired: ErrorScenarioConfig{\n904\t\t\tName:              \"Request Expired\",\n905\t\t\tExpectedCode:      ErrorCodeRequestExpired,\n906\t\t\tExpectedStatus:    403,\n907\t\t\tExpectedMessage:   \"Request has expired\",\n908\t\t\tExpectedKeywords:  []string{\"expired\", \"timestamp\", \"date\"},\n909\t\t\tMinMessageLength:   15,\n910\t\t\tMaxResponseTime:    300 * time.Millisecond,\n911\t\t\tDescription:       \"Tests for requests with expired timestamps. AWS S3 requires requests to be made within a specific time window (typically 15 minutes).\",\n912\t\t\tCategory:          string(CategoryAuth),\n913\t\t},\n914\t}\n915\t\n916\t// =============================================================================\n917\t// AUTHENTICATION ERROR PATTERNS\n918\t// =============================================================================\n919\t// These patterns are specifically designed for authentication-related testing.\n920\t// They cover the spectrum of authentication failures from missing credentials\n921\t// to expired timestamps.\n922\t//\n923\t// Design Philosophy:\n924\t//   - Comprehensive coverage of all auth failure scenarios\n925\t//   - Clear distinction between different auth error types\n926\t//   - Realistic expectations for response times\n927\t//   - Actionable error messages\n928\t//\n929\t// Use these patterns when:\n930\t//   - Testing authentication and authorization logic\n931\t//   - Verifying AWS signature validation\n932\t//   - Testing credential validation\n933\t//   - Writing security-focused tests\n934\t// =============================================================================\n935\t\n936\t// AuthErrorPatterns provides predefined patterns for authentication error scenarios.\n937\t//\n938\t// Use this collection when:\n939\t//   - Testing authentication failures\n940\t//   - Validating AWS signature behavior\n941\t//   - Testing credential validation logic\n942\t//   - Writing security test suites\n943\t//\n944\t// Example:\n945\t//\n946\t//\tpattern := AuthErrorPatterns.MissingAuthHeader\n947\t//\texpectedStatus := pattern.ExpectedStatus    // 403\n948\t//\texpectedCode := pattern.ExpectedCode        // \"MissingAuthenticationToken\"\n949\t//\n950\t// Available patterns:\n951\t//   - MissingAuthHeader: No Authorization header\n952\t//   - InvalidAccessKeyId: Invalid access key\n953\t//   - SignatureDoesNotMatch: Incorrect signature\n954\t//   - MissingDateHeader: No X-Amz-Date header\n955\t//   - RequestExpired: Expired timestamp\n956\t//   - MalformedAuthHeader: Invalid Authorization header format\n957\tvar AuthErrorPatterns = struct {\n958\t\t// MissingAuthHeader covers requests without Authorization header\n959\t\t//\n960\t\t// Use this pattern when:\n961\t\t//   - Testing requests without any authentication\n962\t\t//   - Verifying auth header is required\n963\t\t//   - Testing basic auth enforcement\n964\t\t//\n965\t\t// Expected behavior:\n966\t\t//   - HTTP Status: 403\n967\t\t//   - S3 Code: \"MissingAuthenticationToken\"\n968\t\t//   - Message: \"Missing Authentication Token\"\n969\t\t//   - Response Time: < 250ms\n970\t\tMissingAuthHeader ErrorScenarioConfig\n971\t\n972\t\t// InvalidAccessKeyId covers requests with invalid access key\n973\t\t//\n974\t\t// Use this pattern when:\n975\t\t//   - Testing with non-existent access keys\n976\t\t//   - Verifying key validation logic\n977\t\t//   - Testing typo scenarios\n978\t\t//\n979\t\t// Expected behavior:\n980\t\t//   - HTTP Status: 403\n981\t\t//   - S3 Code: \"InvalidAccessKeyId\"\n982\t\t//   - Message: \"The AWS Access Key Id you provided does not exist\"\n983\t\t//   - Response Time: < 300ms\n984\t\tInvalidAccessKeyId ErrorScenarioConfig\n985\t\n986\t\t// SignatureDoesNotMatch covers requests with incorrect signature\n987\t\t//\n988\t\t// Use this pattern when:\n989\t\t//   - Testing with incorrect secret keys\n990\t\t//   - Verifying signature calculation\n991\t\t//   - Testing signature validation logic\n992\t\t//\n993\t\t// Expected behavior:\n994\t\t//   - HTTP Status: 403\n995\t\t//   - S3 Code: \"SignatureDoesNotMatch\"\n996\t\t//   - Message: \"The request signature we calculated does not match\"\n997\t\t//   - Response Time: < 350ms\n998\t\tSignatureDoesNotMatch ErrorScenarioConfig\n999\t\n1000\t\t// MissingDateHeader covers requests without X-Amz-Date header\n1001\t\t//\n1002\t\t// Use this pattern when:\n1003\t\t//   - Testing signature requirements\n1004\t\t//   - Verifying date header validation\n1005\t\t//   - Testing timestamp enforcement\n1006\t\t//\n1007\t\t// Expected behavior:\n1008\t\t//   - HTTP Status: 403\n1009\t\t//   - S3 Code: \"MissingAuthenticationToken\"\n1010\t\t//   - Message: \"Missing required date header\"\n1011\t\t//   - Response Time: < 250ms\n1012\t\tMissingDateHeader ErrorScenarioConfig\n1013\t\n1014\t\t// RequestExpired covers requests with expired timestamp\n1015\t\t//\n1016\t\t// Use this pattern when:\n1017\t\t//   - Testing stale requests\n1018\t\t//   - Verifying timestamp validation\n1019\t\t//   - Testing time window enforcement\n1020\t\t//\n1021\t\t// Expected behavior:\n1022\t\t//   - HTTP Status: 403\n1023\t\t//   - S3 Code: \"RequestExpired\"\n1024\t\t//   - Message: \"Request has expired\"\n1025\t\t//   - Response Time: < 300ms\n1026\t\tRequestExpired ErrorScenarioConfig\n1027\t\n1028\t\t// MalformedAuthHeader covers requests with invalid Authorization header format\n1029\t\t//\n1030\t\t// Use this pattern when:\n1031\t\t//   - Testing malformed Authorization headers\n1032\t\t//   - Verifying header format validation\n1033\t\t//   - Testing parser robustness\n1034\t\t//\n1035\t\t// Expected behavior:\n1036\t\t//   - HTTP Status: 403\n1037\t\t//   - S3 Code: \"MissingAuthenticationToken\"\n1038\t\t//   - Message: \"Invalid authorization header format\"\n1039\t\t//   - Response Time: < 250ms\n1040\t\tMalformedAuthHeader ErrorScenarioConfig\n1041\t}{\n1042\t\t// MissingAuthHeader: No Authorization header present\n1043\t\tMissingAuthHeader: ErrorScenarioConfig{\n1044\t\t\tName:              \"Missing Authentication Token\",\n1045\t\t\tExpectedCode:      ErrorCodeMissingAuthenticationToken,\n1046\t\t\tExpectedStatus:    403,\n1047\t\t\tExpectedMessage:   \"Missing Authentication Token\",\n1048\t\t\tExpectedKeywords:  []string{\"missing\", \"authentication\", \"token\", \"authorization\"},\n1049\t\t\tMinMessageLength:   15,\n1050\t\t\tMaxResponseTime:    250 * time.Millisecond,\n1051\t\t\tDescription:       \"Tests for requests completely missing the Authorization header. This is the most basic authentication failure scenario.\",\n1052\t\t\tCategory:          string(CategoryAuth),\n1053\t\t},\n1054\t\n1055\t\t// InvalidAccessKeyId: Access key not found or invalid\n1056\t\tInvalidAccessKeyId: ErrorScenarioConfig{\n1057\t\t\tName:              \"Invalid Access Key ID\",\n1058\t\t\tExpectedCode:      ErrorCodeInvalidAccessKeyId,\n1059\t\t\tExpectedStatus:    403,\n1060\t\t\tExpectedMessage:   \"The AWS Access Key Id you provided does not exist in our records\",\n1061\t\t\tExpectedKeywords:  []string{\"access\", \"key\", \"invalid\", \"exist\"},\n1062\t\t\tMinMessageLength:   20,\n1063\t\t\tMaxResponseTime:    300 * time.Millisecond,\n1064\t\t\tDescription:       \"Tests for requests with an invalid or non-existent access key ID. This typically means the key was mistyped or does not belong to any account.\",\n1065\t\t\tCategory:          string(CategoryAuth),\n1066\t\t},\n1067\t\n1068\t\t// SignatureDoesNotMatch: Calculated signature doesn't match\n1069\t\tSignatureDoesNotMatch: ErrorScenarioConfig{\n1070\t\t\tName:              \"Signature Does Not Match\",\n1071\t\t\tExpectedCode:      ErrorCodeSignatureDoesNotMatch,\n1072\t\t\tExpectedStatus:    403,\n1073\t\t\tExpectedMessage:   \"The request signature we calculated does not match the signature you provided\",\n1074\t\t\tExpectedKeywords:  []string{\"signature\", \"match\", \"calculated\", \"provided\"},\n1075\t\t\tMinMessageLength:   30,\n1076\t\t\tMaxResponseTime:    350 * time.Millisecond,\n1077\t\t\tDescription:       \"Tests for requests where the signature calculation failed. This can be due to incorrect secret key, tampered request, or signing algorithm mismatch.\",\n1078\t\t\tCategory:          string(CategoryAuth),\n1079\t\t},\n1080\t\n1081\t\t// MissingDateHeader: Required date header is missing\n1082\t\tMissingDateHeader: ErrorScenarioConfig{\n1083\t\t\tName:              \"Missing Date Header\",\n1084\t\t\tExpectedCode:      ErrorCodeMissingAuthenticationToken,\n1085\t\t\tExpectedStatus:    403,\n1086\t\t\tExpectedMessage:   \"Missing required date header\",\n1087\t\t\tExpectedKeywords:  []string{\"missing\", \"date\", \"header\", \"timestamp\"},\n1088\t\t\tMinMessageLength:   15,\n1089\t\t\tMaxResponseTime:    250 * time.Millisecond,\n1090\t\t\tDescription:       \"Tests for requests missing the X-Amz-Date header. The date header is required for signature calculation and request timestamp validation.\",\n1091\t\t\tCategory:          string(CategoryAuth),\n1092\t\t},\n1093\t\n1094\t\t// RequestExpired: Request timestamp is too old\n1095\t\tRequestExpired: ErrorScenarioConfig{\n1096\t\t\tName:              \"Request Expired\",\n1097\t\t\tExpectedCode:      ErrorCodeRequestExpired,\n1098\t\t\tExpectedStatus:    403,\n1099\t\t\tExpectedMessage:   \"Request has expired\",\n1100\t\t\tExpectedKeywords:  []string{\"expired\", \"timestamp\", \"date\", \"old\"},\n1101\t\t\tMinMessageLength:   15,\n1102\t\t\tMaxResponseTime:    300 * time.Millisecond,\n1103\t\t\tDescription:       \"Tests for requests with timestamps outside the valid window (typically more than 15 minutes old). AWS S3 rejects stale requests.\",\n1104\t\t\tCategory:          string(CategoryAuth),\n1105\t\t},\n1106\t\n1107\t\t// MalformedAuthHeader: Authorization header format is invalid\n1108\t\tMalformedAuthHeader: ErrorScenarioConfig{\n1109\t\t\tName:              \"Malformed Authorization Header\",\n1110\t\t\tExpectedCode:      ErrorCodeMissingAuthenticationToken,\n1111\t\t\tExpectedStatus:    403,\n1112\t\t\tExpectedMessage:   \"Invalid authorization header format\",\n1113\t\t\tExpectedKeywords:  []string{\"authorization\", \"header\", \"malformed\", \"format\", \"invalid\"},\n1114\t\t\tMinMessageLength:   20,\n1115\t\t\tMaxResponseTime:    250 * time.Millisecond,\n1116\t\t\tDescription:       \"Tests for requests with Authorization headers that don't match the expected AWS signature format. Covers missing fields or incorrect structure.\",\n1117\t\t\tCategory:          string(CategoryAuth),\n1118\t\t},\n1119\t}\n1120\t\n1121\t// =============================================================================\n1122\t// CLIENT ERROR PATTERNS (4xx)\n1123\t// =============================================================================\n1124\t// These patterns represent client-side errors (4xx status codes). These are\n1125\t// errors where the client made an invalid request and should correct their\n1126\t// request before retrying.\n1127\t//\n1128\t// Design Philosophy:\n1129\t//   - Clear indication of client-side issues\n1130\t//   - Specific error codes for different client failures\n1131\t//   - Actionable error messages\n1132\t//   - Fast response times for client errors\n1133\t//\n1134\t// Use these patterns when:\n1135\t//   - Testing client error handling\n1136\t//   - Verifying request validation logic\n1137\t//   - Testing API contract compliance\n1138\t//   - Writing integration tests for error scenarios\n1139\t// =============================================================================\n1140\t\n1141\t// ClientErrorPatterns provides predefined patterns for client error scenarios (4xx).\n1142\t//\n1143\t// Use this collection when:\n1144\t//   - Testing 4xx client error responses\n1145\t//   - Validating request validation\n1146\t//   - Testing API behavior for malformed requests\n1147\t//   - Writing comprehensive client error tests\n1148\t//\n1149\t// Example:\n1150\t//\n1151\t//\tpattern := ClientErrorPatterns.NotFound\n1152\t//\texpectedStatus := pattern.ExpectedStatus    // 404\n1153\t//\texpectedCode := pattern.ExpectedCode        // \"NoSuchKey\"\n1154\t//\n1155\t// Available patterns:\n1156\t//   - BadRequest: Generic 400 Bad Request errors\n1157\t//   - NotFound: 404 Not Found errors\n1158\t//   - MethodNotAllowed: 405 Method Not Allowed errors\n1159\t//   - UnsupportedMediaType: 415 Unsupported Media Type errors\n1160\tvar ClientErrorPatterns = struct {\n1161\t\t// BadRequest covers generic 400 Bad Request errors\n1162\t\t//\n1163\t\t// Use this pattern when:\n1164\t\t//   - S3 Code: \"InvalidRequest\"\n1171\t\t//   - Message: \"Bad Request\"\n1172\t\t//   - Response Time: < 200ms\n1173\t\tBadRequest ErrorScenarioConfig\n1174\t\n1175\t\t// NotFound covers 404 Not Found errors\n1176\t\t//\n1177\t\t// Use this pattern when:\n1178\t\t//   - Testing missing resource scenarios\n1179\t\t//   - Verifying 404 error responses\n1180\t\t//   - Testing resource existence validation\n1181\t\t//\n1182\t\t// Expected behavior:\n1183\t\t//   - HTTP Status: 404\n1184\t\t//   - S3 Code: \"NoSuchKey\"\n1185\t\t//   - Response Time: < 500ms\n1186\t\tNotFound ErrorScenarioConfig\n1187\t\n1188\t\t// MethodNotAllowed covers 405 Method Not Allowed errors\n1189\t\t//\n1190\t\t// Use this pattern when:\n1191\t\t//   - Testing HTTP method restrictions\n1192\t\t//   - Verifying method validation\n1193\t\t//   - Testing API endpoint contract\n1194\t\t//\n1195\t\t// Expected behavior:\n1196\t\t//   - HTTP Status: 405\n1197\t\t//   - S3 Code: \"MethodNotAllowed\"\n1198\t\t//   - Response Time: < 200ms\n1199\t\tMethodNotAllowed ErrorScenarioConfig\n1200\t\n1201\t\t// UnsupportedMediaType covers 415 Unsupported Media Type errors\n1202\t\t//\n1203\t\t// Use this pattern when:\n1204\t\t//   - Testing content-type validation\n1205\t\t//   - Verifying media type restrictions\n1206\t\t//   - Testing format enforcement\n1207\t\t//\n1208\t\t// Expected behavior:\n1209\t\t//   - HTTP Status: 415\n1210\t\t//   - S3 Code: \"UnsupportedMediaType\"\n1211\t\t//   - Response Time: < 200ms\n1212\t\tUnsupportedMediaType ErrorScenarioConfig\n1213\t}{\n1214\t\t// BadRequest: Generic bad request error\n1215\t\tBadRequest: ErrorScenarioConfig{\n1216\t\t\tName:              \"Bad Request\",\n1217\t\t\tExpectedCode:      ErrorCodeInvalidRequest,\n1218\t\t\tExpectedStatus:    400,\n1219\t\t\tExpectedMessage:   \"Bad Request\",\n1220\t\t\tExpectedKeywords:  []string{\"bad\", \"request\", \"invalid\"},\n1221\t\t\tMinMessageLength:   10,\n1222\t\t\tMaxResponseTime:    200 * time.Millisecond,\n1223\t\t\tDescription:       \"Tests for generic bad request errors. This is a catch-all for malformed requests that don't fit more specific error categories.\",\n1224\t\t\tCategory:          string(CategoryInvalidRequest),\n1225\t\t},\n1226\t\n1227\t\t// NotFound: Resource not found\n1228\t\tNotFound: CommonErrorPatterns.ResourceNotFound,\n1229\t\n1230\t\t// MethodNotAllowed: HTTP method not supported\n1231\t\tMethodNotAllowed: CommonErrorPatterns.MethodNotAllowed,\n1232\t\n1233\t\t// UnsupportedMediaType: Content type not supported\n1234\t\tUnsupportedMediaType: CommonErrorPatterns.UnsupportedMediaType,\n1235\t}\n1236\t\n1237\t// =============================================================================\n1238\t// SERVER ERROR PATTERNS (5xx)\n1239\t// =============================================================================\n1240\t// These patterns represent server-side errors (5xx status codes). These are\n1241\t// errors where the server failed to fulfill a valid request.\n1242\t//\n1243\t// Design Philosophy:\n1244\t//   - Identify server-side issues requiring investigation\n1245\t//   - Provide actionable error information\n1246\t//   - InternalError: 500 Internal Server Error\n1272\t//   - ServiceUnavailable: 503 Service Unavailable\n1273\tvar ServerErrorPatterns = struct {\n1274\t\t// InternalError covers 500 Internal Server Error\n1275\t\t//\n1276\t\t// Use this pattern when:\n1277\t\t//   - S3 Code: \"InternalError\"\n1284\t\t//   - Message: \"Internal server error\"\n1285\t\t//   - Response Time: < 1000ms\n1286\t\tInternalError ErrorScenarioConfig\n1287\t\n1288\t\t// ServiceUnavailable covers 503 Service Unavailable\n1289\t\t//\n1290\t\t// Use this pattern when:\n1291\t\t//   - HTTP Status: 503\n1298\t\t//   - S3 Code: \"InternalError\"\n1299\t\t//   - Message: \"Service Unavailable\"\n1300\t\t//   - Response Time: < 2000ms\n1301\t\tServiceUnavailable ErrorScenarioConfig\n1302\t}{\n1303\t\t// InternalError: Generic internal server error\n1304\t\tInternalError: CommonErrorPatterns.InternalServerError,\n1305\t\n1306\t\t// ServiceUnavailable: Service temporarily unavailable\n1307\t\tServiceUnavailable: ErrorScenarioConfig{\n1308\t\t\tName:              \"Service Unavailable\",\n1309\t\t\tExpectedCode:      ErrorCodeInternalError,\n1310\t\t\tExpectedStatus:    503,\n1311\t\t\tExpectedMessage:   \"Service Unavailable\",\n1312\t\t\tExpectedKeywords:  []string{\"unavailable\", \"service\", \"temporarily\"},\n1313\t\t\tMinMessageLength:   15,\n1314\t\t\tMaxResponseTime:    2000 * time.Millisecond,\n1315\t\t\tDescription:       \"Tests for service unavailability errors. This typically occurs during maintenance, overload, or infrastructure issues.\",\n1316\t\t\tCategory:          string(CategoryInternal),\n1317\t\t},\n1318\t}\n1319\t\n1320\t// =============================================================================\n1321\t// ERROR PATTERN COLLECTIONS BY CATEGORY\n1322\t// errors.\n1325\t// =============================================================================\n1326\t\n1327\t// ErrorPatternByCategory maps error categories to their predefined patterns.\n1328\t// This allows quick access to all patterns for a specific error type.\n1329\tvar ErrorPatternByCategory = map[ErrorCategory][]ErrorScenarioConfig{\n1330\t\tCategoryAuth: {\n1331\t\t\tAuthErrorPatterns.MissingAuthHeader,\n1332\t\t\tAuthErrorPatterns.InvalidAccessKeyId,\n1333\t\t\tAuthErrorPatterns.SignatureDoesNotMatch,\n1334\t\t\tAuthErrorPatterns.MissingDateHeader,\n1335\t\t\tAuthErrorPatterns.RequestExpired,\n1336\t\t\tAuthErrorPatterns.MalformedAuthHeader,\n1337\t\t\tCommonErrorPatterns.SignatureMismatch,\n1338\t\t},\n1339\t\tCategoryNotFound: {\n1340\t\t\tClientErrorPatterns.NotFound,\n1341\t\t},\n1342\t\tCategoryInvalidRequest: {\n1343\t\t\tClientErrorPatterns.BadRequest,\n1344\t\t\tClientErrorPatterns.UnsupportedMediaType,\n1345\t\t\tCommonErrorPatterns.InvalidRequest,\n1346\t\t},\n1347\t\tCategoryMethodNotAllowed: {\n1348\t\t\tClientErrorPatterns.MethodNotAllowed,\n1349\t\t},\n1350\t\tCategoryInternal: {\n1351\t\t\tServerErrorPatterns.InternalError,\n1352\t\t\tServerErrorPatterns.ServiceUnavailable,\n1353\t\t},\n1354\t}\n1355\t\n1356\t// =============================================================================\n1357\t// ERROR PATTERN BUILDERS\n1358\t// =============================================================================\n1359\t// These builder functions help create custom error patterns based on\n1360\t// predefined patterns or from scratch.\n1361\t//\n1362\t// Use these functions when:\n1363\t//   - Getting patterns by error code dynamically\n1364\t//   - Retrieving all patterns for a specific category\n1365\t//   - Getting all common patterns for iteration\n1366\t//   - Building custom validation logic\n1367\t// =============================================================================\n1368\t\n1369\t// PatternForCode returns a predefined pattern for the given error code,\n1370\t// or a default pattern if no specific pattern exists.\n1371\t//\n1372\t// This function provides dynamic access to predefined patterns based on their\n1373\t// error code. It's useful when working with error codes from responses or\n1374\t// external sources.\n1375\t//\n1376\t// Parameters:\n1377\t//   - code: S3 error code (e.g., \"NoSuchKey\", \"AccessDenied\")\n1378\t//\n1379\t// Returns:\n1380\t//   - ErrorScenarioConfig for the given error code, or a default pattern\n1381\t//\n1382\t// Use this function when:\n1383\t//   - Looking up patterns dynamically from error codes\n1384\t//   - Handling errors from external sources\n1385\t//   - Building flexible validation logic\n1386\t//   - Converting error codes to test patterns\n1387\t//\n1388\t// Example:\n1389\t//\n1390\t//\tpattern := PatternForCode(\"NoSuchKey\")\n1391\t//\tfmt.Printf(\"Pattern: %s, Status: %d\\n\", pattern.Name, pattern.ExpectedStatus)\n1392\t//\n1393\t// Example (unknown code):\n1394\t//\n1395\t//\tpattern := PatternForCode(\"UnknownError\")\n1396\t//\t// Returns default pattern with ExpectedStatus: 500\n1397\t//\n1398\t// Supported error codes:\n1399\t//   - \"AccessDenied\" → CommonErrorPatterns.AccessDenied\n1400\t//   - \"InvalidAccessKeyId\" → AuthErrorPatterns.InvalidAccessKeyId\n1401\t//   - \"SignatureDoesNotMatch\" → AuthErrorPatterns.SignatureDoesNotMatch\n1402\t//   - \"MissingAuthenticationToken\" → AuthErrorPatterns.MissingAuthHeader\n1403\t//   - \"NoSuchKey\" → CommonErrorPatterns.ResourceNotFound\n1404\t//   - \"MethodNotAllowed\" → CommonErrorPatterns.MethodNotAllowed\n1405\t//   - \"UnsupportedMediaType\" → CommonErrorPatterns.UnsupportedMediaType\n1406\t//   - \"InvalidRequest\" → CommonErrorPatterns.InvalidRequest\n1407\t//   - \"InternalError\" → CommonErrorPatterns.InternalServerError\n1408\t//   - \"RequestExpired\" → CommonErrorPatterns.RequestExpired\n1409\t//   - Any other code → Default pattern (500, \"Unknown Error Pattern\")\n1410\tfunc PatternForCode(code string) ErrorScenarioConfig {\n1411\t\tswitch code {\n1412\t\tcase ErrorCodeAccessDenied:\n1413\t\t\treturn CommonErrorPatterns.AccessDenied\n1414\t\tcase ErrorCodeInvalidAccessKeyId:\n1415\t\t\treturn AuthErrorPatterns.InvalidAccessKeyId\n1416\t\tcase ErrorCodeSignatureDoesNotMatch:\n1417\t\t\treturn AuthErrorPatterns.SignatureDoesNotMatch\n1418\t\tcase ErrorCodeMissingAuthenticationToken:\n1419\t\t\treturn AuthErrorPatterns.MissingAuthHeader\n1420\t\tcase ErrorCodeNoSuchKey:\n1421\t\t\treturn CommonErrorPatterns.ResourceNotFound\n1422\t\tcase ErrorCodeMethodNotAllowed:\n1423\t\t\treturn CommonErrorPatterns.MethodNotAllowed\n1424\t\tcase ErrorCodeUnsupportedMediaType:\n1425\t\t\treturn CommonErrorPatterns.UnsupportedMediaType\n1426\t\tcase ErrorCodeInvalidRequest:\n1427\t\t\treturn CommonErrorPatterns.InvalidRequest\n1428\t\tcase ErrorCodeInternalError:\n1429\t\t\treturn CommonErrorPatterns.InternalServerError\n1430\t\tcase ErrorCodeRequestExpired:\n1431\t\t\treturn CommonErrorPatterns.RequestExpired\n1432\t\tdefault:\n1433\t\t\t// Return a default pattern for unknown codes\n1434\t\t\treturn ErrorScenarioConfig{\n1435\t\t\t\tName:             \"Unknown Error Pattern\",\n1436\t\t\t\tExpectedCode:     code,\n1437\t\t\t\tExpectedStatus:   500,\n1438\t\t\t\tExpectedMessage:  \"Unknown error\",\n1439\t\t\t\tMinMessageLength: 10,\n1440\t\t\t\tMaxResponseTime:  1000 * time.Millisecond,\n1441\t\t\t\tDescription:      \"Default pattern for unknown error codes\",\n1442\t\t\t\tCategory:         string(CategoryGeneral),\n1443\t\t\t}\n1444\t\t}\n1445\t}\n1446\t\n1447\t// PatternsForCategory returns all predefined patterns for a given error category.\n1448\t//\n1449\t// This function provides access to all patterns belonging to a specific category,\n1450\t// useful for organized testing and validation by error type.\n1451\t//\n1452\t// Parameters:\n1453\t//   - category: Error category (e.g., CategoryAuth, CategoryNotFound)\n1454\t//\n1455\t// Returns:\n1456\t//   - Slice of ErrorScenarioConfig for the category, or empty slice if not found\n1457\t//\n1458\t// Use this function when:\n1459\t//   - Testing all errors of a specific type\n1460\t//   - Building category-specific test suites\n1461\t//   - Organizing tests by error category\n1462\t//   - Validating category-based error handling\n1463\t//\n1464\t// Example:\n1465\t//\n1466\t//\tauthPatterns := PatternsForCategory(CategoryAuth)\n1467\t//\tfor _, pattern := range authPatterns {\n1468\t//\t    fmt.Printf(\"Auth pattern: %s\\n\", pattern.Name)\n1469\t//\t}\n1470\t//\n1471\t// Example (unknown category):\n1472\t//\n1473\t//\tpatterns := PatternsForCategory(\"UnknownCategory\")\n1474\t//\t// Returns empty slice\n1475\t//\n1476\t// Available categories:\n1477\t//   - CategoryAuth: 7 patterns (authentication errors)\n1478\t//   - CategoryNotFound: 1 pattern (resource not found)\n1479\t//   - CategoryInvalidRequest: 3 patterns (invalid requests)\n1480\t//   - CategoryMethodNotAllowed: 1 pattern (method restrictions)\n1481\t//   - CategoryInternal: 2 patterns (server errors)\n1482\tfunc PatternsForCategory(category ErrorCategory) []ErrorScenarioConfig {\n1483\t\tpatterns, exists := ErrorPatternByCategory[category]\n1484\t\tif !exists {\n1485\t\t\treturn []ErrorScenarioConfig{}\n1486\t\t}\n1487\t\tresult := make([]ErrorScenarioConfig, len(patterns))\n1488\t\tcopy(result, patterns)\n1489\t\treturn result\n1490\t}\n1491\t\n1492\t// AllCommonPatterns returns all predefined common error patterns.\n1493\t//\n1494\t// This function provides access to all common error patterns in a single slice,\n1495\t// useful for comprehensive testing and iteration over all common scenarios.\n1496\t//\n1497\t// Returns:\n1498\t//   - Slice of all 8 common error patterns\n1499\t//\n1500\t// Use this function when:\n1501\t//   - Testing all common error scenarios\n1502\t//   - Building comprehensive test suites\n1503\t//   - Iterating over all common patterns\n1504\t//   - Validating common error handling\n1505\t//\n1506\t// Example:\n1507\t//\n1508\t//\tallPatterns := AllCommonPatterns()\n1509\t//\tfor _, pattern := range allPatterns {\n1510\t//\t    fmt.Printf(\"Pattern: %s (Status: %d)\\n\", pattern.Name, pattern.ExpectedStatus)\n1511\t//\t}\n1512\t//\n1513\t// Example (count):\n1514\t//\n1515\t//\ttotalPatterns := len(AllCommonPatterns()) // 8\n1516\t//\n1517\t// Returned patterns (in order):\n1518\t//   1. ResourceNotFound (404)\n1519\t//   2. AccessDenied (403)\n1520\t//   3. InvalidRequest (400)\n1521\t//   4. UnsupportedMediaType (415)\n1522\t//   5. MethodNotAllowed (405)\n1523\t//   6. InternalServerError (500)\n1524\t//   7. SignatureMismatch (403)\n1525\t//   8. RequestExpired (403)\n1526\tfunc AllCommonPatterns() []ErrorScenarioConfig {\n1527\t\treturn []ErrorScenarioConfig{\n1528\t\t\tCommonErrorPatterns.ResourceNotFound,\n1529\t\t\tCommonErrorPatterns.AccessDenied,\n1530\t\t\tCommonErrorPatterns.InvalidRequest,\n1531\t\t\tCommonErrorPatterns.UnsupportedMediaType,\n1532\t\t\tCommonErrorPatterns.MethodNotAllowed,\n1533\t\t\tCommonErrorPatterns.InternalServerError,\n1534\t\t\tCommonErrorPatterns.SignatureMismatch,\n1535\t\t\tCommonErrorPatterns.RequestExpired,\n1536\t\t}\n1537\t}\n1538\t\n1539\t// =============================================================================\n1540\t// DOCUMENTATION\n1541\t// =============================================================================\n1542\t\n1543\t/*\n1544\tFile Organization:\n1545\t\n1546\tThis file (error_test_patterns.go) contains:\n1547\t- Base error types (S3Error, ErrorResponseFixture)\n1548\t- Error configuration types (ErrorScenarioConfig)\n1549\t- Error categorization (ErrorCategory, error codes)\n1550\t- Mapping functions (CategoryForCode, ExpectedStatusCodeForCode)\n1551\t- Predefined error patterns (CommonErrorPatterns, AuthErrorPatterns, etc.)\n1552\t\n1553\tRelated files:\n1554\t- error_test_infrastructure_test.go - Test-specific helpers and validators\n1555\t- error_test_patterns_base_test.go - Test pattern structures and tables\n1556\t- error_test_patterns_test.go - Tests for the pattern infrastructure\n1557\t- http_error_fixtures.go - HTTP error response fixtures\n1558\t\n1559\tUsage Patterns:\n1560\t\n1561\t1. Using predefined patterns:\n1562\t   pattern := CommonErrorPatterns.ResourceNotFound\n1563\t   config := pattern.Config()\n1564\t\n1565\t2. Getting a pattern by error code:\n1566\t   pattern := PatternForCode(\"NoSuchKey\")\n1567\t\n1568\t3. Getting all patterns for a category:\n1569\t   authPatterns := PatternsForCategory(CategoryAuth)\n1570\t\n1571\t4.
