Package server provides configurable test server infrastructure for error testing.\n//\n// # Error Server Setup\n//\n// This file provides types and functions for setting up test servers with\n// configurable error responses. These are used across multiple test files.\n//\n// Usage:\n//   scenarios := []ErrorServerScenario{\n//       {StatusCode: 404, ErrorCode: \"NoSuchKey\", Message: \"Not found\"},\n//   }\n//   server := NewConfigurableErrorServer(scenarios)\n//   defer server.Close()\npackage server\n\nimport (\n\t\"encoding/xml\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"io\"\n\t\"net/http\"\n\t\"net/http/httptest\"\n\t\"strings\"\n\t\"testing\"\n\t\"time\"\n)\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR SCENARIO CONFIGURATION\n// =============================================================================\n\n// ErrorServerScenario defines the configuration for an error response scenario.\ntype ErrorServerScenario struct {\n\t// StatusCode is the HTTP status code to return\n\tStatusCode int\n\n\t// ErrorCode is the S3 error code to include in the response\n\tErrorCode string\n\n\t// Message is the error message\n\tMessage string\n\n\t// Resource is the resource path that caused the error (optional)\n\tResource string\n\n\t// Headers are additional HTTP headers to include (optional)\n\tHeaders map[string]string\n\n\t// Delay is the artificial delay before responding (useful for testing timeouts)\n\tDelay time.Duration\n\n\t// BodyOverride is a custom response body (optional, overrides automatic XML generation)\n\tBodyOverride string\n\n\t// RequestMatcher is a function to match incoming requests (optional)\n\t// If nil, all requests will match this scenario\n\tRequestMatcher func(*http.Request) bool\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// TEST SERVER SETUP\n// =============================================================================\n\n// ConfigurableErrorServer is a test server that returns configured error responses.\ntype ConfigurableErrorServer struct {\n\t// Server is the underlying httptest server\n\tServer *httptest.Server\n\n\t// URL is the base URL of the test server\n\tURL string\n\n\t// Scenarios is the list of error scenarios in priority order\n\tScenarios []ErrorServerScenario\n\n\t// DefaultScenario is used when no other scenario matches\n\tDefaultScenario *ErrorServerScenario\n\n\t// RequestCount tracks the number of requests handled\n\tRequestCount int\n\n\t// RequestLog contains a log of all requests received\n\tRequestLog []LoggedRequest\n\n\t// ResponseLog contains a log of all responses sent\n\tResponseLog []LoggedResponse\n}\n\n// LoggedRequest represents a logged incoming request.\ntype LoggedRequest struct {\n\tTimestamp time.Time\n\tMethod    string\n\tPath      string\n\tHeaders   http.Header\n\tBody      string\n}\n\n// LoggedResponse represents a logged outgoing response.\ntype LoggedResponse struct {\n\tTimestamp   time.Time\n\tStatusCode  int\n\tHeaders     http.Header\n\tBody        string\n\tScenarioIdx int // Index of the scenario that generated this response\n}\n\n// NewConfigurableErrorServer creates a new test server with configurable error responses.\n//\n// This helper function creates an HTTP test server that returns configured error responses\n// based on the provided scenarios. It's useful for testing client error handling.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - scenarios: List of error scenarios (evaluated in order, first match wins)\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - *ConfigurableErrorServer: Configured test server\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tscenarios := []ErrorServerScenario{\n//\t    {StatusCode: 404, ErrorCode: \"NoSuchKey\", Message: \"Not found\"},\n//\t    {StatusCode: 403, ErrorCode: \"AccessDenied\", Message: \"Access denied\"},\n//\t}\n//\tserver := NewConfigurableErrorServer(scenarios)\n//\tdefer server.Close()\n//\n//\t// Make requests to server.URL\nfunc NewConfigurableErrorServer(scenarios []ErrorServerScenario) *ConfigurableErrorServer {\n\ts := &ConfigurableErrorServer{\n\t\tScenarios:  scenarios,\n\t\tRequestLog: make([]LoggedRequest, 0),\n\t\tResponseLog: make([]LoggedResponse, 0),\n\t}\n\n\thandler := http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\t\ts.handleRequest(w, r)\n\t})\n\n\ts.Server = httptest.NewServer(handler)\n\ts.URL = s.Server.URL\n\treturn s\n}\n\n// NewConfigurableErrorServerWithDefault creates a server with a default scenario.\n//\n// This variant creates a server that uses a default scenario when no other scenarios match.\n// Useful for testing with a primary error case and fallback behavior.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - scenarios: List of error scenarios (evaluated in order)\n//   - defaultScenario: Fallback scenario when no other matches\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - *ConfigurableErrorServer: Configured test server\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tdefaultScenario := ErrorServerScenario{\n//\t    StatusCode: 500,\n//\t    ErrorCode: \"InternalError\",\n//\t    Message: \"Internal server error\",\n//\t}\n//\tserver := NewConfigurableErrorServerWithDefault(scenarios, &defaultScenario)\n//\tdefer server.Close()\nfunc NewConfigurableErrorServerWithDefault(scenarios []ErrorServerScenario, defaultScenario *ErrorServerScenario) *ConfigurableErrorServer {\n\ts := NewConfigurableErrorServer(scenarios)\n\ts.DefaultScenario = defaultScenario\n\treturn s\n}\n\n// handleRequest processes an incoming request and responds with the appropriate error.\nfunc (s *ConfigurableErrorServer) handleRequest(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\ts.RequestCount++\n\n\t// Log the request\n\ts.logRequest(r)\n\n\t// Find matching scenario\n\tscenario, scenarioIdx := s.findMatchingScenario(r)\n\n\t// Apply delay if configured\n\tif scenario.Delay > 0 {\n\t\ttime.Sleep(scenario.Delay)\n\t}\n\n\t// Set custom headers FIRST (before WriteHeader)\n\tfor key, value := range scenario.Headers {\n\t\tw.Header().Set(key, value)\n\t}\n\n\t// Set default Content-Type if not set\n\tif w.Header().Get(\"Content-Type\") == \"\" {\n\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Set status code LAST (after headers)\n\tw.WriteHeader(scenario.StatusCode)\n\n\t// Write response body\n\tbody := s.getResponseBody(scenario)\n\tw.Write([]byte(body))\n\n\t// Log the response\n\ts.logResponse(scenario, scenarioIdx, body)\n}\n\n// findMatchingScenario finds the first scenario that matches the request.\nfunc (s *ConfigurableErrorServer) findMatchingScenario(r *http.Request) (ErrorServerScenario, int) {\n\tfor i, scenario := range s.Scenarios {\n\t\tif scenario.RequestMatcher == nil || scenario.RequestMatcher(r) {\n\t\t\treturn scenario, i\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Use default if no match\n\tif s.DefaultScenario != nil {\n\t\treturn *s.DefaultScenario, -1\n\t}\n\n\t// Fallback to 500 error\n\treturn ErrorServerScenario{\n\t\tStatusCode: 500,\n\t\tErrorCode:  \"InternalError\",\n\t\tMessage:    \"No matching scenario found\",\n\t}, -1\n}\n\n// getResponseBody generates the response body for a scenario.\nfunc (s *ConfigurableErrorServer) getResponseBody(scenario ErrorServerScenario) string {\n\t// Use custom body if provided\n\tif scenario.BodyOverride != \"\" {\n\t\treturn scenario.BodyOverride\n\t}\n\n\t// Generate S3 error XML\n\ts3Err := S3ErrorResponse{\n\t\tCode:     scenario.ErrorCode,\n\t\tMessage:  scenario.Message,\n\t\tResource: scenario.Resource,\n\t\tRequestId: fmt.Sprintf(\"req-%d\", s.RequestCount),\n\t}\n\n\t// Add additional fields from headers if they exist\n\tif method, ok := scenario.Headers[\"X-Error-Method\"]; ok {\n\t\ts3Err.Method = method\n\t}\n\tif allowedMethods, ok := scenario.Headers[\"X-Error-Allowed-Methods\"]; ok {\n\t\ts3Err.AllowedMethods = allowedMethods\n\t}\n\tif contentType, ok := scenario.Headers[\"X-Error-Content-Type\"]; ok {\n\t\ts3Err.ContentType = contentType\n\t}\n\n\txml, err := ToXMLWithError(s3Err)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"<?xml version=\\\"1.0\\\"?><Error><Code>InternalError</Code><Message>Failed to generate error response: %v</Message></Error>\", err)\n\t}\n\n\treturn xml\n}\n\n// ToXMLWithError converts the S3 error response to XML format with error handling.\n// This is a helper function for generating S3 XML responses.\nfunc ToXMLWithError(e S3ErrorResponse) (string, error) {\n\te.XMLName = xml.Name{Local: \"Error\"}\n\tbytes, err := xml.Marshal(e)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn \"\", err\n\t}\n\treturn xml.Header + string(bytes), nil\n}\n\n// logRequest logs an incoming request.\nfunc (s *ConfigurableErrorServer) logRequest(r *http.Request) {\n\tbodyBytes, _ := io.ReadAll(r.Body)\n\tr.Body.Close()\n\n\tloggedReq := LoggedRequest{\n\t\tTimestamp: time.Now(),\n\t\tMethod:    r.Method,\n\t\tPath:      r.URL.Path,\n\t\tHeaders:   r.Header.Clone(),\n\t\tBody:      string(bodyBytes),\n\t}\n\n\ts.RequestLog = append(s.RequestLog, loggedReq)\n}\n\n// logResponse logs an outgoing response.\nfunc (s *ConfigurableErrorServer) logResponse(scenario ErrorServerScenario, scenarioIdx int, body string) {\n\tloggedResp := LoggedResponse{\n\t\tTimestamp:   time.Now(),\n\t\tStatusCode:  scenario.StatusCode,\n\t\tHeaders:     make(http.Header),\n\t\tBody:        body,\n\t\tScenarioIdx: scenarioIdx,\n\t}\n\n\t// Copy headers\n\tfor key, value := range scenario.Headers {\n\t\tloggedResp.Headers.Set(key, value)\n\t}\n\tloggedResp.Headers.Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n\n\ts.ResponseLog = append(s.ResponseLog, loggedResp)\n}\n\n// Close closes the test server.\nfunc (s *ConfigurableErrorServer) Close() {\n\ts.Server.Close()\n}\n\n// Reset resets the request count and logs.\nfunc (s *ConfigurableErrorServer) Reset() {\n\ts.RequestCount = 0\n\ts.RequestLog = make([]LoggedRequest, 0)\n\ts.ResponseLog = make([]LoggedResponse, 0)\n}\n\n// GetLastRequest returns the most recent request log.\nfunc (s *ConfigurableErrorServer) GetLastRequest() *LoggedRequest {\n\tif len(s.RequestLog) == 0 {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\treturn &s.RequestLog[len(s.RequestLog)-1]\n}\n\n// GetLastResponse returns the most recent response log.\nfunc (s *ConfigurableErrorServer) GetLastResponse() *LoggedResponse {\n\tif len(s.ResponseLog) == 0 {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\treturn &s.ResponseLog[len(s.ResponseLog)-1]\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// REQUEST MATCHERS\n// =============================================================================\n\n// MatchByPath creates a request matcher that matches by URL path.\nfunc MatchByPath(path string) func(*http.Request) bool {\n\treturn func(r *http.Request) bool {\n\t\treturn r.URL.Path == path\n\t}\n}\n\n// MatchByPathPrefix creates a request matcher that matches by path prefix.\nfunc MatchByPathPrefix(prefix string) func(*http.Request) bool {\n\treturn func(r *http.Request) bool {\n\t\treturn strings.HasPrefix(r.URL.Path, prefix)\n\t}\n}\n\n// MatchByMethod creates a request matcher that matches by HTTP method.\nfunc MatchByMethod(method string) func(*http.Request) bool {\n\treturn func(r *http.Request) bool {\n\t\treturn r.Method == method\n\t}\n}\n\n// MatchByMethodAndPath creates a request matcher that matches both method and path.\nfunc MatchByMethodAndPath(method, path string) func(*http.Request) bool {\n\treturn func(r *http.Request) bool {\n\t\treturn r.Method == method && r.URL.Path == path\n\t}\n}\n\n// MatchByHeader creates a request matcher that matches by header value.\nfunc MatchByHeader(header, value string) func(*http.Request) bool {\n\treturn func(r *http.Request) bool {\n\t\treturn r.Header.Get(header) == value\n\t}\n}\n\n// MatchAlways creates a request matcher that always matches.\nfunc MatchAlways() func(*http.Request) bool {\n\treturn func(r *http.Request) bool {\n\t\treturn true\n\t}\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// CONVENIENCE FUNCTIONS\n//        tt.errorType,\n\t\t\t\tMessageOverride:  tt.overrideMsg,\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tgot := scenario.getDefaultMessage()\n\t\t\tif !contains(got, tt.expectedSubstr) =============================================================================\n// TESTS FOR ENHANCED ERROR SERVER\n// =============================================================================\n\nfunc TestNewEnhancedErrorServer(t *testing.T) {\n\tt.Run(\"creates enhanced server successfully\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\tscenarios := []EnhancedErrorScenario{\n\t\t\tNewRateLimitScenario(0.5, 5*time.Second),\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tserver := NewEnhancedErrorServer(scenarios)\n\t\tdefer server.Close()\n\n\t\tif server == nil {\n\t\t\tt.Fatal(\"Expected non-nil server\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif server.ConfigurableErrorServer == nil {\n\t\t\tt.Error(\"Expected base server to be initialized\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif len(server.EnhancedScenarios) != 1 {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected 1 scenario, got %d\", len(server.EnhancedScenarios))\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif server.RandomSource == nil {\n\t\t\tt.Error(\"Expected random source to be initialized\")\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\n\tt.Run(\"creates server with empty scenarios\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\tscenarios := []EnhancedErrorScenario{}\n\n\t\tserver := NewEnhancedErrorServer(scenarios)\n\t\tdefer server.Close()\n\n\t\tif server == nil {\n\t\t\tt.Fatal(\"Expected non-nil server\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif len(server.EnhancedScenarios) != 0 {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected 0 scenarios, got %d\", len(server.EnhancedScenarios))\n\t\t}\n\t})\n}\n\nfunc TestNewEnhancedErrorServerWithSeed(t *testing.T) {\n\tt.Run(\"creates server with deterministic randomness\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\tscenarios := []EnhancedErrorScenario{\n\t\t\tNewTimeoutScenario(1.0, 100*time.Millisecond),\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tseed := int64(12345)\n\t\tserver := NewEnhancedErrorServerWithSeed(scenarios, seed)\n\t\tdefer server.Close()\n\n\t\tif server.RandomSource == nil {\n\t\t\tt.Fatal(\"Expected random source to be initialized\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Test that randomness is deterministic\n\t\tfirstValue := server.RandomSource.Float64()\n\t\tsecondValue := server.RandomSource.Float64()\n\n\t\tif firstValue == secondValue {\n\t\t\tt.Error(\"Random source should produce different values\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Create another server with same seed and verify first value matches\n\t\tserver2 := NewEnhancedErrorServerWithSeed(scenarios, seed)\n\t\tdefer server2.Close()\n\n\t\tfirstValue2 := server2.RandomSource.Float64()\n\t\tif firstValue != firstValue2 {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected same first value with same seed: %f != %f\", firstValue, firstValue2)\n\t\t}\n\t})\n}\n\nfunc TestEnhancedErrorServer_ShouldInjectError(t *testing.T) {\n\tt.Run(\"respects frequency setting\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\tscenarios := []EnhancedErrorScenario{\n\t\t\tNewRateLimitScenario(1.0, 5*time.Second), // 100% frequency\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tserver := NewEnhancedErrorServerWithSeed(scenarios, 999)\n\t\tdefer server.Close()\n\n\t\treq, _ := http.NewRequest(\"GET\", \"/test\", nil)\n\n\t\t// With 100% frequency, should always inject\n\t\tfor i := 0; i < 10; i++ {\n\t\t\tif !server.ShouldInjectError(scenarios[0], req) {\n\t\t\t\tt.Error(\"Expected error to be injected with 100% frequency\")\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\ttotal, errors, rate := server.GetStats()\n\t\tif errors != 10 {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected 10 errors, got %d\", errors)\n\t\t}\n\t\tif rate != 1.0 {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected 100%% error rate, got %f\", rate)\n\t\t}\n\t\tif total != 10 {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected 10 total requests, got %d\", total)\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\n\tt.Run(\"respects zero frequency\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\tscenarios := []EnhancedErrorScenario{\n\t\t\tNewRateLimitScenario(0.0, 5*time.Second), // 0% frequency\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tserver := NewEnhancedErrorServerWithSeed(scenarios, 999)\n\t\tdefer server.Close()\n\n\t\treq, _ := http.NewRequest(\"GET\", \"/test\", nil)\n\n\t\t// With 0% frequency, should never inject\n\t\tfor i := 0; i < 10; i++ {\n\t\t\tif server.ShouldInjectError(scenarios[0], req) {\n\t\t\t\tt.Error(\"Expected no error injection with 0% frequency\")\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t_, errors, rate := server.GetStats()\n\t\tif errors != 0 {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected 0 errors, got %d\", errors)\n\t\t}\n\t\tif rate != 0.0 {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected 0%% error rate, got %f\", rate)\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\n\tt.Run(\"respects condition function\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\tscenarios := []EnhancedErrorScenario{\n\t\t\tNewRateLimitScenario(1.0, 5*time.Second),\n\t\t}\n\t\tscenarios[0].Condition = func(r *http.Request) bool {\n\t\t\treturn r.URL.Path == \"/trigger\"\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tserver := NewEnhancedErrorServerWithSeed(scenarios, 999)\n\t\tdefer server.Close()\n\n\t\treq1, _ := http.NewRequest(\"GET\", \"/trigger\", nil)\n\t\treq2, _ := http.NewRequest(\"GET\", \"/other\", nil)\n\n\t\tif !server.ShouldInjectError(scenarios[0], req1) {\n\t\t\tt.Error(\"Expected error injection for matching path\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif server.ShouldInjectError(scenarios[0], req2) {\n\t\t\tt.Error(\"Expected no error injection for non-matching path\")\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\n\tt.Run(\"respects intercept flag\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\tscenarios := []EnhancedErrorScenario{\n\t\t\tNewRateLimitScenario(0.0, 5*time.Second), // 0% frequency\n\t\t}\n\t\tscenarios[0].Intercept = true\n\n\t\tserver := NewEnhancedErrorServer(scenarios)\n\t\tdefer server.Close()\n\n\t\treq, _ := http.NewRequest(\"GET\", \"/test\", nil)\n\n\t\t// With intercept=true, should inject regardless of frequency\n\t\tif !server.ShouldInjectError(scenarios[0], req) {\n\t\t\tt.Error(\"Expected error injection with intercept flag\")\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\n\tt.Run(\"combines frequency and probability\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\tscenarios := []EnhancedErrorScenario{\n\t\t\tNewRateLimitScenario(0.5, 5*time.Second),\n\t\t}\n\t\tscenarios[0].Probability = 0.5 // Both frequency and probability\n\n\t\tserver := NewEnhancedErrorServer(scenarios)\n\t\tdefer server.Close()\n\n\t\treq, _ := http.NewRequest(\"GET\", \"/test\", nil)\n\n\t\t// With both set at 0.5, actual injection should be around 0.25 (0.5 * 0.5)\n\t\t// We'll test 1000 times and check that it's reasonable\n\t\tinjections := 0\n\t\tfor i := 0; i < 1000; i++ {\n\t\t\tif server.ShouldInjectError(scenarios[0], req) {\n\t\t\t\tinjections++\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\trate := float64(injections) / 1000.0\n\t\t// Should be around 0.25, give it some tolerance (0.20 to 0.30)\n\t\tif rate < 0.20 || rate > 0.30 {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected injection rate around 0.25, got %f\", rate)\n\t\t}\n\t})\n}\n\nfunc TestEnhancedErrorServer_GetStats(t *testing.T) {\n\tt.Run(\"tracks statistics correctly\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\tscenarios := []EnhancedErrorScenario{\n\t\t\tNewRateLimitScenario(0.5, 5*time.Second),\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tserver := NewEnhancedErrorServer(scenarios)\n\t\tdefer server.Close()\n\n\t\treq, _ := http.NewRequest(\"GET\", \"/test\", nil)\n\n\t\t// Make 10 requests\n\t\tfor i := 0; i < 10; i++ {\n\t\t\tserver.ShouldInjectError(scenarios[0], req)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\ttotal, errors, rate := server.GetStats()\n\n\t\tif total != 10 {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected 10 total requests, got %d\", total)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// With 50% frequency, errors should be around 5\n\t\tif errors < 3 || errors > 7 {\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Warning: Expected around 5 errors, got %d (acceptable variance)\", errors)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Rate should be around 0.5\n\t\tif rate < 0.3 || rate > 0.7 {\n\t\t\tt.Logf(\"Warning: Expected rate around 0.5, got %f (acceptable variance)\", rate)\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\n\tt.Run(\"returns zero stats initially\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\tscenarios := []EnhancedErrorScenario{\n\t\t\tNewRateLimitScenario(0.5, 5*time.Second),\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tserver := NewEnhancedErrorServer(scenarios)\n\t\tdefer server.Close()\n\n\t\ttotal, errors, rate := server.GetStats()\n\n\t\tif total != 0 {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected 0 total requests, got %d\", total)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif errors != 0 {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected 0 errors, got %d\", errors)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif rate != 0.0 {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected 0.0 rate, got %f\", rate)\n\t\t}\n\t})\n}\n\nfunc TestEnhancedErrorServer_ResetCounters(t *testing.T) {\n\tt.Run(\"resets counters correctly\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\tscenarios := []EnhancedErrorScenario{\n\t\t\tNewRateLimitScenario(1.0, 5*time.Second),\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tserver := NewEnhancedErrorServer(scenarios)\n\t\tdefer server.Close()\n\n\t\treq, _ := http.NewRequest(\"GET\", \"/test\", nil)\n\n\t\t// Make some requests\n\t\tfor i := Test Server Setup Helper for Error Scenarios - Implementation Summary\n\n## Task Completion\n\nBead: bf-456ve2 - Implement test server setup helper for error scenarios\n\n## Acceptance Criteria Status\n\n✅ **All acceptance criteria met:**\n\n1. ✅ **Create a helper function that sets up a test server with error injection capabilities**\n   - `NewEnhancedErrorServer()` - Advanced error injection server\n   - `NewConfigurableErrorServer()` - Base configurable error server\n   - `NewEnhancedErrorServerWithSeed()` - Deterministic variant for reproducible tests\n\n2. ✅ **Support common error scenarios (timeout, connection refused, rate limit, etc.)**\n   - Timeout errors: `ErrorTypeTimeout` with configurable delay\n   - Connection refused: `ErrorTypeConnectionRefused` (simulated via 503)\n   - Rate limiting: `ErrorTypeRateLimit` with retry-after headers\n   - Service unavailable: `ErrorTypeServiceUnavailable`\n   - Internal errors: `ErrorTypeInternalError`\n   - Bad request: `ErrorTypeBadRequest`\n   - Unauthorized/Forbidden: `ErrorTypeUnauthorized`, `ErrorTypeForbidden`\n   - Not found: `ErrorTypeNotFound`\n   - Slow responses: `ErrorTypeSlowResponse`\n\n3. ✅ **Ensure the helper integrates with existing test infrastructure**\n   - Extends base `ConfigurableErrorServer`\n   - Uses existing `ErrorServerScenario` types\n   - Compatible with existing request matchers\n   - Integrates with S3 error response structures\n\n4. ✅ **Add configuration options for error type, severity, and frequency**\n   - `EnhancedErrorScenario.Type` - Error category\n   - `EnhancedErrorScenario.Severity` - Impact level (low/medium/high/critical)\n   - `EnhancedErrorScenario.Frequency` - How often errors occur (0.0-1.0)\n   - `EnhancedErrorScenario.Probability` - Per-request probability\n   - `EnhancedErrorScenario.RetryAfter` - Retry duration\n   - `EnhancedErrorScenario.Condition` - Conditional injection function\n   - `EnhancedErrorScenario.Intercept` - Request interception flag\n\n5. ✅ **Include basic unit tests verifying server setup**\n   - 15+ test functions covering all helper functions\n   - Tests for convenience functions (rate limit, timeout, etc.)\n   - Tests for severity-based scenarios\n   - Tests for enhanced server functionality\n   - Integration tests with real HTTP requests\n   - All tests passing ✅\n\n## Implementation scenario builders\n\n2. **`internal/server/error_server_enhanced_test.go`** (809 lines)\n   - Comprehensive test coverage\n   - Tests for all convenience functions\n   - Tests for server behavior and Control\n\n```go\n// Frequency-based injection (30% of requests)\nscenario := NewRateLimitScenario(0.3, 5*time.Second)\n\n// Deterministic with seed\nserver := NewEnhancedErrorServerWithSeed(scenarios, 12345)\n\n// Conditional injection\nscenario.Condition = func(r *http.Request) bool {\n    return r.URL.Path == \"/trigger\"\n}\n```\n\n### Statistical Tracking\n\n```go\ntotal, errors, rate := server.GetStats()\n// Returns: (100, 30, 0.3) for 30% error rate\n\nserver.ResetCounters() // Reset for new test phase\n```\n\n### Preconfigured Scenarios\n\n```go\n// Use preconfigured scenarios\nscenario := PreconfiguredEnhancedScenarios.OccasionalRateLimit\n\n// Build custom scenarios\nscenario := NewRateLimitScenario(0.5, 10*time.Second)\nscenario.Severity = ErrorSeverityHigh\n```\n\n## Testing Results\n\nAll tests passing (30.253s total):\n- ✅ Enhanced error server tests (7 test functions)\n- ✅ Convenience function Example\n\n```go\npackage server_test\n\nimport (\n    \"testing\"\n    \"time\"\n    \".github.com/jedarden/armor/internal/server\"\n)\n\nfunc TestMyClientWithRateLimit(t *testing.T) {\n    scenarios := []EnhancedErrorScenario{\n        {\n            Type:      ErrorTypeRateLimit,\n            Severity:  ErrorSeverityMedium,\n            Frequency: 0.3, // 30% of requests\n            RetryAfter:
